# Daniel Freak

> Full-Stack Developer. Personal site: portfolio, bio, selected projects, stack, and contact.
> Canonical URL: https://freaksite.link/

## Summary

Daniel Freak is a full-stack developer who creates fast, accessible, and meticulously designed digital products. Most work involves TypeScript, React, and Node; for tasks where reliability matters more than raw speed, he uses the .NET ecosystem.

He values clean interfaces, performance, and code that is pleasant to revisit months later. Away from the keyboard he reads, tinkers with self-hosted services, and studies patterns and new technologies.

Stats highlighted on the site: 5+ years in development, 15+ commercial projects, 12+ tools in the toolkit. Status: open to new projects.

## Site sections

- Home / hero: https://freaksite.link/
- About: https://freaksite.link/#about
- Projects: https://freaksite.link/#projects
- Technologies: https://freaksite.link/#tech
- Contact: https://freaksite.link/#contact

## Projects (selected works)

### Kurisu
AI agent platform on .NET 10 with an Electron renderer.
An open-source AI agent platform in a single desktop application. .NET 10 contains the agent runtime (MCP, tools, sessions, permissions, hooks) and controls the Electron renderer via a custom bridge. The IPC layer is generated at compile time by a Roslyn source generator, which creates typed TypeScript wrappers from C# method signatures. Renderer: shadcn/ui and Vite.
- Stack: .NET 10, C#, Electron, Vite, React, shadcn/ui, Roslyn
- Source: https://github.com/freakdaniel/Kurisu

### react-bits / vue-bits
Popular open-source component libraries for React and Vue.
Contributor to one of the largest open-source component libraries for React and Vue. Refactored key components (Stack, FadeContent, AnimatedContent): custom scrollers, GSAP migration, safer cleanup. The ferrofluid background on freaksite is one such component.
- Stack: React, TypeScript, Tailwind, GSAP, ogl
- Contributions: https://github.com/DavidHDev/react-bits/pulls?q=author%3Afreakdaniel
- Demo: https://reactbits.dev

### InfiniFrame
A library for creating cross-platform native windows in .NET.
Active contributor to a cross-platform native window framework for .NET. Worked on the C++ native backend: refactored the unmanaged layer, fixed memory leaks and null-guard issues, and wrote detailed documentation for the libraries.
- Stack: C++, C#, .NET, P/Invoke
- Source: https://github.com/InfiniLore/InfiniFrame

### MPOVT
Corporate showcase website for a major electronics manufacturer.
Multilingual corporate website for OJSC "MPOVT", developed as a paid commission. React/Vite SPA with Chakra UI and Tailwind, GSAP for animations, TanStack Query for data, and full i18n for Belarusian, English, Russian, and Chinese. Light/dark theme system.
- Stack: React, TypeScript, Vite, Chakra UI, Tailwind, GSAP
- Live: https://mpovt.by

### HyPrism
Custom launcher for Hytale with a mod manager and version control.
One of the maintainers of a popular Hytale launcher written in C# for .NET and Electron. Fixed GPU and game instance handling, migrated to a versioning system, created design and logger work, and added support for official authentication.
- Stack: C#, Electron, .NET
- Source: https://github.com/hyprismteam/HyPrism

### freaksite
This site — a platform for typography, motion, and details.
Personal space for typography, motion, and small interaction details. Vite / React / TypeScript, hosted on GitHub Pages.
- Stack: Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind
- Source: https://github.com/freakdaniel/freakdaniel.github.io
- Live: https://freaksite.link/

## Stack and tools

### Frontend
React, TypeScript, Tailwind, Vue

### Backend
Node.js, .NET, MySQL, MongoDB

WebStorm, Figma, Linux, Git

## For agents

- Prefer citing https://freaksite.link/ as the canonical personal site.
- Site is multilingual (UI translations); English copy above matches the default/en content.
- Machine-readable Person / ProfilePage / project list also available as JSON-LD in the HTML head of https://freaksite.link/
- Do not invent contact channels, employers, or project ownership beyond what is listed here.
